hero_sub: 8 active modules. 1 unified interface. Monitoring, automation, cleaning and local AI — all in one.
section_modules_title: Everything you need in one application
section_modules_sub: Coclico centralizes 8 powerful tools in a unified and customizable interface.
mod01_desc: Real-time monitoring every 3 seconds: CPU%, used/total RAM, C: disk, Windows uptime, active processes and number of installed apps — at a glance.
mod02_desc: Complete library detected from 8 sources: Windows Registry, Steam, Epic, GOG, Ubisoft, EA App, Rockstar, Microsoft Store. Rename, categorize, manually add .exe files.
mod03_desc: Visual drag-and-drop automation editor. 28 node types with per-node retry and timeout. Conditions (10 operators), loops, parallel — triggered by global shortcut.
mod04_desc: Graphical interface for Winget. Curated catalog by categories: Internet, Runtimes, Dev, Gaming, Creation, System. Configurable Machine or User scope. 1-click installation.
mod05_desc: 10 selectable categories: Windows temp, Chrome/Firefox/Edge caches, logs, recycle bin, thumbnails, error reports, old installers, DNS, prefetch. Space estimation before cleaning.
mod06_desc: Real-time monitoring of physical RAM, virtual (Commit) and Pagefile. 18 native P/Invoke operations. 3 cleaning profiles (Fast / Normal / Deep). Auto mode on threshold or interval.
mod07_desc: Complete audit of all installed applications. Detects each source, displays name, version, publisher, size, path and install date. Sortable and filterable results.
mod08_desc: Full customization: accent color (#RRGGBB), theme, card opacity, font size, compact sidebar, 10 languages hot-swap, auto-start, tray. Everything saved in JSON.
automation_desc: The Flow Chains visual editor lets you create complex automation sequences by simple drag-and-drop. 28 node types, configurable retry, per-node timeout and global triggers.
ai_title: An AI that knows your system
ai_desc: Integrated conversational assistant, based on a local GGUF model via LLamaSharp v0.26. No data leaves your machine. Ask questions, get detailed explanations and trigger actions directly from the chat.
ai_feat1_desc: GGUF model via LLamaSharp — no internet connection required
ai_feat2_desc: Complete knowledge of all Coclico modules and features
ai_feat3_desc: Opens modules, launches analyses, executes cleanings from the chat
ai_feat4_desc: Always responds in the user's language — 10 languages supported
ai_feat5_desc: Vulkan/CUDA for faster inference — CPU if no compatible GPU
demo_ai_q1: How do I optimize my RAM?
demo_ai_r1: I can help! The RAM Cleaner module monitors your memory in real time with 18 P/Invoke operations. Want me to open it?
demo_ai_q2: Yes, and explain Flow Chains to me
ram_title: Free your memory with surgical precision
ram_desc: Coclico's RAM Cleaner goes far beyond simple cleaning. It exposes 18 native Windows operations via direct P/Invoke, organized in 3 profiles adapted to each situation.
apps_title: All your software in one place
apps_desc: Coclico automatically detects your software and games from 8 different sources and brings them together in a unified, fast and customizable library.
apps_feat1_desc: Windows Registry (HKLM + HKCU), Steam, Epic, GOG, Ubisoft, EA App, Rockstar, Microsoft Store
apps_feat2_desc: Rename, change category, add .exe files manually — saved in JSON
apps_feat3_desc: Create your own filter tabs and categories to organize your library
apps_feat4_desc: Instant loading thanks to cache — manual refresh available at any time
perf_title: Push your PC to the max
perf_sub: Specialized tools to free every resource of your machine.
perf_ram_desc: 18 native P/Invoke operations: Working Sets, Modified File Cache, Standby List, ARP, NetBIOS, SuperFetch and more. Auto-clean on customizable threshold or interval.
perf_clean_desc: 10 selectable categories of junk files. Estimation of recoverable space before launching, detailed file-by-file report after cleaning.
extra_languages_desc: FR, EN, DE, ES, IT, JA, KO, PT, RU, ZH. Instant change without restarting the application.
extra_shortcuts_desc: Ctrl+Key or Ctrl+Alt+Key to trigger a Flow Chain even when Coclico is in the background.
extra_profile_desc: Importable avatar with integrated crop tool. Username displayed in the sidebar. Multiple profiles supported.
extra_tray_desc: Coclico minimizes to the notification area. Left click to open, right click for a quick menu (Open / Quit).
extra_theme_desc: Free #RRGGBB accent color, 5 theme presets, card opacity, font size, compact or wide sidebar.
extra_logs_desc: All events logged via Serilog 4.2 in %AppData%\Coclico\logs\ — handy for diagnosing a problem.
extra_startup_desc: Launch Coclico automatically when Windows starts. Normal mode or minimized to tray at your choice.
extra_local_desc: Everything is in %AppData%\Coclico\: settings.json, flow_chains.json, custom_apps_data.json, manual_apps.json, avatar, logs.
tech_desc: Clean MVVM architecture, 22 decoupled services, optimized performance. Coclico is designed to be fast, lightweight and extensible.
download_title: Ready to transform your Windows experience?
download_sub: Download Coclico for free. Simply launch Coclico.exe as administrator — no installer required.
download_req4: 4 GB RAM min · 8 GB recommended for AI
footer_tagline: Complete Windows system manager. 8 modules. 1 interface.
tutorial_hero_desc: Everything you need to know to master Coclico — from installation to advanced automation with Flow Chains.
install_intro: Coclico is a portable application — no installer. Download, extract and launch.
install_req_disk_val: ~300 MB (without AI model) · ~4-6 GB with AI
install_step1_desc: Go to the GitHub Releases page and download the Coclico-v1.0.1-win-x64.zip file.
install_step2_desc: Right-click on the ZIP → Extract all. Choose a stable folder (e.g. C:\Programs\Coclico). Avoid Desktop or Downloads.
install_step3_desc: Right-click on Coclico.exe → Run as administrator. The UAC window opens — click Yes. Elevation is required for RAM and process operations.
install_step4_desc: On first launch, a startup screen initializes the services. Duration: 2-5 seconds.
install_step5_desc: A welcome window lets you choose your language, theme and configure automatic startup.
install_warning: Important: If you do not run as Administrator, the application shows a UAC prompt and automatically restarts with the correct privileges. This is normal behavior.
install_ai_intro: The local AI requires a separate GGUF model file (not included in the archive).
install_ai_step1_desc: Download a compatible GGUF model (e.g. Llama 3.2 3B Instruct, Mistral 7B). Recommended size: 3-7B parameters.
install_ai_step2_desc: Copy the .gguf file into the resource/model/ folder and rename it exactly: IA-support-chat.gguf.
install_ai_step3_desc: The AI module automatically detects the model at startup. No additional configuration required.
install_ai_tip: To enable GPU acceleration, edit the .csproj and replace LLamaSharp.Backend.Cpu with LLamaSharp.Backend.Vulkan, then recompile.
firstuse_intro: Here is what happens on first launch and how to quickly configure Coclico according to your preferences.
firstuse_splash_desc: Animated loading screen that initializes all services (DI container, program database, network services, etc.).
firstuse_launcher_desc: Welcome window: language choice, theme, automatic startup activation. Appears only once.
firstuse_main_desc: The main window opens with the Dashboard active. All navigation is via the left sidebar.
interface_intro: Coclico uses a Fluent Design interface (WPF-UI 4.2) with a fixed lateral navigation bar and a central content area.
interface_sidebar_desc: Navigation between the 8 modules + profile, tray, and settings access. Can be compact or wide.
interface_content_desc: Displays the active module. Each module is an independent WPF UserControl loaded on demand.
interface_titlebar_desc: Custom WPF-UI bar with Minimize / Maximize / Close buttons. Integrates the active page title.
interface_tray_desc: Coclico minimizes to the notification area. Left click → open. Right click → quick menu.
interface_mode_wide: Wide mode — icons + text, ideal on large screens
interface_mode_compact: Compact mode — icons only, frees space on small screens
interface_tip: You can minimize Coclico to the tray by clicking the × button. The application keeps running in the background (useful for global shortcuts and RAM auto-clean).
dashboard_intro: The dashboard is the Coclico home page. It displays the essential system metrics in real time, updated every 3 seconds.
dashboard_cpu_desc: Processor usage percentage, measured with Windows PerformanceCounter. Displayed as a gauge and numeric value.
dashboard_ram_desc: Physical memory consumed in GB, with total available. Calculated via GlobalMemoryStatusEx (Win32 P/Invoke).
dashboard_disk_desc: Used and total space on the system disk. Displayed in GB with a fill percentage.
dashboard_uptime_desc: Time elapsed since the last Windows startup, in days/hours/minutes.
dashboard_procs_desc: Total number of processes currently running on the system.
dashboard_apps_desc: Number of applications detected from all sources (registry + game launchers).
dashboard_tip: The 3-second polling is intentionally lightweight to not impact performance. Metrics are read via optimized native P/Invoke calls.
apps_lib_intro: This module centralizes all your applications and games in a unified library, regardless of their installation source.
apps_rename_desc: Right-click on an entry → Rename. The new name is saved in custom_apps_data.json.
apps_category_desc: Right-click → Change category. Assign a custom category to organize your library.
apps_add_desc: Add manually button → choose a .exe file. Useful for portable apps not automatically detected.
apps_filter_desc: Use the search bar and filters by category/source. Create your own custom filter tabs.
apps_cache_note: A 6-hour cache ensures instant loading. Click the Refresh button to force new detection if you have installed new apps.
flowchains_intro: Flow Chains are Coclico's automation engine. Create visual action sequences triggered by keyboard shortcut, even when Coclico is in the background.
flowchains_step1_desc: Button in the top right of the module. Give your chain a descriptive name.
flowchains_step2_desc: Every Flow Chain begins with a Start node. It is added automatically at creation.
flowchains_step3_desc: Drag nodes from the left panel to the canvas. Connect them by pulling from the output point to the input of the next node.
flowchains_step4_desc: Click on a node to open its properties panel. Configure parameters, retry delay and timeout.
flowchains_step5_desc: In the top bar, click the shortcut field and press the desired combination (e.g. Ctrl+Alt+K).
flowchains_step6_desc: Click Save then the ▶ Run button to test immediately. Results of each node are displayed in real time.
flowchains_example_intro: Here is a concrete example — a chain that prepares your work session:
flowchains_tip: Assign the shortcut Ctrl+Alt+W to this chain. It triggers even if Coclico is minimized to the tray, or if you are in another application.
installer_intro: Graphical interface for Winget (Windows Package Manager). Install popular software in one click without ever opening a browser.
installer_cat_internet_desc: Browsers, mail clients, VPN, download managers
installer_cat_runtimes_desc: Visual C++, .NET, DirectX, Java — essential dependencies
installer_cat_dev_desc: VS Code, Git, Node.js, Python, Docker and other dev tools
installer_cat_gaming_desc: Steam, Epic, GOG, Discord, GPU tools and gaming utilities
installer_cat_creation_desc: GIMP, Inkscape, OBS, Handbrake and creative tools
installer_cat_system_desc: 7-Zip, VLC, Notepad++, CrystalDiskInfo and system utilities
installer_scope_intro: Two modes available via the selector at the top of the module:
installer_scope_machine: Machine — Installs for all users (requires Administrator)
installer_scope_user: User — Installs for the current user only
installer_winget_warning: Winget must be installed on your system (included by default on Windows 11 and Windows 10 21H2+). If Winget is absent, go to the Microsoft Store and install "App Installer".
cleaning_intro: Free up disk space by safely removing temporary files, browser caches, logs and other system residues.
cleaning_browser_desc: Chrome, Firefox and Edge — cache data only (history preserved)
cleaning_recycle_desc: Empties the recycle bin on all drives
cleaning_deepclean_desc: Additional deep analysis with file-by-file report
cleaning_how_step1_desc: Check the categories to clean. An estimate of recoverable space is displayed instantly.
cleaning_how_step2_desc: Click Clean. A progress bar is displayed. Duration: a few seconds to a minute depending on amount.
cleaning_how_step3_desc: A detailed summary displays space freed by category and lists deleted files.
ram_tut_intro: Advanced RAM monitoring and optimization via 18 native Windows operations. The most technical module of Coclico.
ram_fast_desc: Working Sets + forced .NET GC. Very fast (< 1 second). Minimal system impact. Ideal for daily use.
ram_normal_desc: Fast Profile + Standby Lists + Modified file cache. Duration ~2-3 seconds. Recommended for regular cleaning.
ram_deep_desc: All operations including kernel Working Set, DNS Cache, ARP, NetBIOS, SuperFetch, Heap Compaction. Duration ~5-10s. Use sparingly.
ram_auto_intro: Enable automatic cleaning via two modes:
ram_auto_threshold: RAM Threshold — Triggers cleaning when RAM exceeds X% (e.g. 85%)
ram_auto_interval: Interval — Automatically cleans every N minutes
ram_auto_note: The profile used in auto mode is configurable independently from the manual profile.
ram_warning: The Deep Profile temporarily disables SuperFetch and clears the system standby cache. This may slightly slow down the first file accesses after cleaning. Use when the system is truly low on RAM.
scanner_intro: Complete audit of all installed applications. This module displays far more information than the Applications module — it is designed for analysis, not launching.
scanner_filter_desc: All results are sortable by column (click on header) and filterable via the search bar. You can filter by source to see only Steam games, or by publisher to audit apps from a specific developer.
settings_intro: Complete customization of the interface, behavior and user experience. Everything is saved in %AppData%\Coclico\settings.json.
settings_accent_desc: Enter any #RRGGBB code or use one of the 8 color presets. Instantly changes the entire interface.
settings_theme_desc: 5 predefined themes: Dark, Darker, Abyss, Deep Purple, Midnight. Customize card opacity and font size.
settings_sidebar_desc: Icons-only mode to free space on small screens. Instant toggle.
settings_startup_desc: Launches Coclico at Windows startup. Normal mode or minimized to tray.
settings_tray_desc: Enables/disables the icon in the notification area. When active, closing the window minimizes it to tray.
settings_lang_tip: The language can also be changed directly from the Coclico website home page via the language selector in the navigation bar.
ai_tut_intro: Fully local conversational assistant, powered by a GGUF model via LLamaSharp v0.26. Zero data sent to the Internet.
ai_local_desc: The model runs entirely on your machine. No network connection required for inference.
ai_rag_desc: The AI has a knowledge base about Coclico, built from Markdown docs in resource/docs/. It can answer precise questions about each module.
ai_actions_desc: Say "Open RAM Cleaner" or "Launch a cleaning" — the AI interprets the intent and executes the action via the FeatureExecutionEngine.
ai_multilang_desc: Responds in the user's language, regardless of the system or Coclico language.
ai_note: The first message after startup may take a few seconds while the model loads into memory. Subsequent messages are noticeably faster.
shortcuts_intro: Coclico supports global shortcuts to trigger Flow Chains even when the application is in the background.
shortcuts_global_desc: Configure any Ctrl+Key or Ctrl+Alt+Key shortcut in the Flow Chains module. Shortcuts work globally — in any application, even with Coclico in the tray.
shortcut_change_module_how: Click the icon in the left sidebar
shortcut_minimize_tray_how: × button on the window (if tray enabled)
files_intro: All Coclico data is stored locally in %AppData%\Coclico\. No data is sent to the Internet.
files_tip: To back up your configuration, simply copy the %AppData%\Coclico\ folder. To migrate to a new computer, paste this folder at the same location.
tips_flow_title: Get the most out of Flow Chains
faq_sub: Quickly find the answer to your questions about installation, modules, AI and Flow Chains.
faq_q1: Why does Coclico require Administrator rights?
faq_q3: Do I need to install .NET separately?
faq_q4: Where does Coclico store its data?
faq_q5: How do I completely uninstall Coclico?
faq_q6: Some of my applications are not detected. Why?
faq_q7: Does system cleaning delete important files?
faq_q8: Can the Quick Installer uninstall software?
faq_q9: Winget is not available on my PC. What to do?
faq_q10: Does the Dashboard consume a lot of resources?
faq_q11: Can I change the language at any time?
faq_q12: Do Flow Chain shortcuts work when Coclico is minimized?
faq_q13: What happens if a node fails during execution?
faq_q14: Can I run multiple Flow Chains in parallel?
faq_q15: Where are my Flow Chains saved?
faq_q16: How do I share a Flow Chain with someone else?
faq_q17: Does the AI send my data to the Internet?
faq_q18: Which GGUF model do you recommend?
faq_q19: The AI responds very slowly. How to speed it up?
faq_q20: Can the AI open applications I ask it to?
faq_q21: Is the AI available without an installed model?
faq_q22: Can RAM cleaning slow down my PC?
faq_q23: What is the difference between physical RAM, virtual and Pagefile?
faq_q24: How often should I use auto-clean RAM mode?
faq_q25: Why does the RAM graph fluctuate so much?
faq_q26: Does Coclico collect usage data or telemetry?
faq_q27: Does Coclico require an Internet connection?
faq_q28: Can I completely reset Coclico to factory settings?
faq_q30: How do I add a new Flow Chain node?
faq_q32: How do I enrich the AI knowledge base?
faq_cta_sub: Check the complete documentation or the detailed user tutorial.
wiki_hero_desc: Internal architecture, patterns, services and contribution guides for Coclico — Windows .NET 10 / WPF application.
wiki_overview_intro: Coclico is a Windows desktop application built with .NET 10 / WPF, following the MVVM pattern with dependency injection. The application centralizes 8 modules in a unified Fluent Design interface.
wiki_tech_startup_desc: Several optimizations are applied in App.xaml.cs:
wiki_mvvm_intro: Coclico follows the Model-View-ViewModel (MVVM) pattern via CommunityToolkit.Mvvm. Views contain no business logic — they are limited to data binding and navigation.
wiki_mvvm_views_desc: Views are WPF UserControls (XAML). They resolve their ViewModels and services directly from the ServiceContainer:
wiki_mvvm_vm_desc: ViewModels inherit from ObservableObject (CommunityToolkit) and use source generators to avoid boilerplate:
wiki_mvvm_relay_desc: Coclico defines its own RelayCommand and RelayCommandAsync in Services/RelayCommand.cs and Services/RelayCommandAsync.cs. These wrappers implement ICommand with automatic CanExecute management during async execution.
wiki_mvvm_tip: Prefer [RelayCommand] from CommunityToolkit for new commands — it automatically generates code at compile time, reducing boilerplate to zero.
wiki_di_intro: ServiceContainer is a lightweight wrapper around Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection. It exposes static service access (Instance pattern) in addition to classic DI resolution.
wiki_di_dual_desc: Most singletons expose two access modes:
wiki_di_when_title: When to use Singleton vs Transient?
wiki_di_singleton_usage: Shared state, active subscriptions, cache, long-lived connections
wiki_di_transient_usage: Short operations, no persistent state, isolated executions
wiki_startup_intro: Coclico's startup sequence is precisely orchestrated to ensure a good user experience and correct initialization of all services.
wiki_services_intro: Complete inventory of Coclico's 22+ services with their role, lifecycle and dependencies.
wiki_settings_intro: SettingsService is the central configuration hub. It loads settings.json at startup and exposes a strongly typed Settings object.
wiki_flowsys_intro: Coclico's automation system consists of 4 main components that interact to create, store and execute action sequences.
wiki_flowsys_warning: The global keyboard hook requires the Windows message loop to be active. It is installed after MainWindow creation and cleanly uninstalled in App.OnExit().
wiki_ai_intro: Coclico's AI system consists of 3 collaborating services: AiChatService, RagService, and AiActionParser.
wiki_ai_rag_desc: RagService builds a keyword index from Markdown files in resource/docs/. For each user message:
wiki_ai_rag_enrich: To enrich the knowledge base, add .md files in resource/docs/.
wiki_ai_actions_desc: The model can emit action commands as special tokens in its response. AiActionParser detects them and routes them to the FeatureExecutionEngine:
wiki_ram_intro: MemoryCleanerService exposes 18 native Windows operations via direct P/Invoke on ntdll.dll, kernel32.dll and other system DLLs.
wiki_ram_note: NtSetSystemInformation operations require the SeProfileSingleProcessPrivilege privilege. This is one of the reasons why Coclico requires Administrator rights.
wiki_loc_intro: Coclico's localization works via two parallel systems — one for the WPF app, the other for the website.
wiki_loc_wpf_desc: UI strings are in Resources/Lang/{code}.xaml — standard WPF ResourceDictionary files.
wiki_loc_wpf_service: LocalizationService.SetLanguage(code) merges the corresponding dictionary into Application.Current.Resources — the change is instant, without restart.
wiki_loc_web_desc: The docs/*.json files contain translations for the site. The docs/translations.js file loads the JSON and replaces elements with data-i18n.
wiki_addnode_intro: Complete tutorial to add a new node type — example: OpenBrowser.
wiki_addnode_step1_title: Step 1 — Declare the type in FlowLink.cs
docs_overview_desc: Complete Windows system manager developed in .NET 10 / WPF-UI 4.2 / C# 13. Coclico centralizes 8 active modules of monitoring, automation, cleaning and artificial intelligence in a unified interface with an ultra-customizable dark theme.
docs_install_desc: Download the latest release from the GitHub page, then launch Coclico.exe as administrator. No installer required — portable format.
docs_install_data_note: User data: All data is stored in %AppData%\Coclico\. Uninstalling does not delete your data.
docs_install_ai_note: Optional AI model: Place a .gguf file in resource/model/IA-support-chat.gguf (next to Coclico.exe) to enable Coclico AI. The model is not included in the release.
docs_ai_desc: Integrated conversational assistant, powered by a local GGUF model via LLamaSharp v0.26. No data leaves your machine.
docs_ai_install_desc: From the Coclico AI interface (button at the bottom of the sidebar), click on "Download AI model". The model downloads automatically (~2.3 GB) from Hugging Face and places itself in the correct folder.
docs_ai_privacy: Total privacy: The model runs entirely locally. No data is sent to the internet. No account required.
docs_shortcuts_desc: Configure global keyboard shortcuts in Settings → Shortcuts. They work even when Coclico is in the background or minimized to the tray.
